posts.title: Hello, world
posts.author: anon
posts.excerpt: First post. This is a scaffold — delete me and write something real.
posts.body:
# Hello

This is the scaffolded first post. Edit `data/posts.json` to add your own.

posts.title: Writing technical posts that ship
posts.author: anon
posts.excerpt: Three rules for posts engineers actually finish.
posts.body:
# Writing technical posts that ship

1. Start from a concrete problem you hit.
2. Timebox the draft to one sitting.
3. Ship the title before the body.

The rest is editing.

posts.title: Small tools, big leverage
posts.author: anon
posts.excerpt: A tiny CLI beats a big framework when the shape isn't stable yet.
posts.body:
# Small tools, big leverage

The shape of the problem isn't clear until you've solved it once. A tiny CLI lets you hold the whole loop in your head while you find that shape.

posts.title: On scaffolds — reference implementations vs templates
posts.author: anon
posts.excerpt: Scaffolds are documentation you can execute.
posts.body:
# On scaffolds

A good scaffold teaches a shape. The reader copies it, breaks it, fixes it — and in the fixing, internalizes the invariants.
